papers.title: Targeting modulated vascular smooth muscle cells in atherosclerosis via FAP-directed immunotherapy
papers.abstract: Vascular smooth muscle cell (VSMC) diversification drives atherosclerotic coronary artery disease (CAD). Mechanisms governing these cell state transitions remain unclear. We applied multiomic single-cell profiling, epitope mapping, and spatial transcriptomics across 27 human coronary arteries, identifying fibroblast activation protein (FAP) as a marker of modulated VSMCs. Lineage tracing in mice indicated that FAP+ cells originate from Myh11+ VSMCs, and FAP PET imaging in CAD patients showed plaque uptake. FAP+ cells states resided in the macrophage-rich neo-intima. Therapeutically, we developed an anti-FAP bispecific T-cell engager, which reduced plaque burden and remodeled the stromal-immune microenvironment through T-cell clonal expansion. Our study delivers a single-cell and spatial atlas of human CAD, establishes FAP as a marker of modulated VSMCs, and highlights immunotherapy for lipid-independent targets.

papers.llm_title_zh: 靶向动脉粥样硬化中调节型血管平滑肌细胞的FAP导向免疫治疗
papers.llm_title_bilingual: 靶向动脉粥样硬化中调节型血管平滑肌细胞的FAP导向免疫治疗 / Targeting modulated vascular smooth muscle cells in atherosclerosis via FAP-directed immunotherapy
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 在动脉粥样硬化性冠状动脉疾病中，血管平滑肌细胞状态转变由何种细胞群体和分子标志所表征，以及是否可通过靶向FAP阳性调节型VSMC的免疫治疗来减轻斑块负荷并重塑病灶微环境？
llm_highlights_zh: 多组学单细胞谱系分析与空间转录组学在27份人冠状动脉样本中鉴定出FAP为调节型血管平滑肌细胞的标志物。
小鼠谱系追踪显示FAP阳性细胞来源于Myh11阳性血管平滑肌细胞，且FAP PET在冠状动脉疾病患者斑块中显示摄取信号。
抗FAP双特异性T细胞连接器可降低斑块负荷，并通过促进T细胞克隆扩增重塑基质-免疫微环境。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 本研究聚焦动脉粥样硬化中调节型血管平滑肌细胞的识别与治疗靶向，提出FAP可作为相关细胞状态的标志，并探索FAP导向免疫治疗的应用潜力。
papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 血管平滑肌细胞（VSMC）的多样化推动了动脉粥样硬化性冠状动脉疾病（CAD）的发生发展，但调控这些细胞状态转变的机制仍不清楚。研究者对27份人冠状动脉样本开展多组学单细胞分析、表位定位和空间转录组学研究，鉴定出成纤维细胞活化蛋白（FAP）是调节型VSMC的标志物。小鼠谱系追踪显示，FAP阳性细胞来源于Myh11阳性VSMC；在CAD患者中，FAP PET成像可见斑块摄取。FAP阳性细胞状态主要位于富含巨噬细胞的新内膜区域。在治疗方面，研究构建了一种抗FAP双特异性T细胞连接器，可降低斑块负荷，并通过诱导T细胞克隆扩增重塑基质-免疫微环境。该研究构建了人类CAD的单细胞与空间图谱，确立了FAP作为调节型VSMC标志物的意义，并提示针对非脂质靶点的免疫治疗具有潜力。

papers.title: Concurrent L1 retrotransposition events promote reciprocal translocations in human tumorigenesis
papers.abstract: LINE-1 (L1) retrotransposition generates somatic genomic variation in human cancer, but short-read sequencing has limited our understanding of its structural consequences and dynamics. Using long-read sequencing, we analyzed 10 tumors with exceptionally high retrotransposition activity, comprising over 6,000 somatic events. We reveal that L1-mediated reciprocal translocations occur frequently, typically driven by two concurrent L1 retrotransposition events on non-homologous chromosomes. Using an independent tumor cohort spanning low to high L1 activity, we estimate that retrotransposon-mediated rearrangements arise at a frequency of one event per 60 somatic retrotranspositions. Molecular timing analyses indicate that these events arise early in tumorigenesis, establishing L1 activity as an early driver of chromosomal instability. Our findings demonstrate that L1 contributes substantially to cancer genome evolution in certain tumors.

papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: LINE-1（L1）逆转座可在人类癌症中产生体细胞基因组变异，但短读长测序限制了我们对其结构后果及动态过程的认识。研究采用长读长测序分析了10例逆转座活性异常高的肿瘤，共包含6,000余个体细胞事件。结果显示，L1介导的相互易位频繁发生，通常由位于非同源染色体上的两个并发L1逆转座事件所驱动。基于一个独立的、涵盖低至高L1活性的肿瘤队列，研究估算逆转座子介导的重排发生频率约为每60个体细胞逆转座事件中出现1次。分子时序分析提示，这类事件在肿瘤发生早期即已出现，从而确立了L1活性作为染色体不稳定性早期驱动因素的作用。研究结果表明，在某些肿瘤中，L1对癌症基因组演化具有重要贡献。

papers.title: A SWI/SNF-specific Ig-like domain, SWIFT, is a transcription factor binding platform
papers.abstract: Mammalian SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complexes modulate DNA accessibility and gene expression; however, their genomic targeting mechanisms remain incompletely understood. Here, we identify SWIFT (SWI/SNF Ig-fold for transcription factor interactions), a conserved transcription factor (TF) binding platform on the SMARCD subunits. SWIFT is necessary and sufficient for direct engagement with the transactivation domain of PU.1. A single amino acid mutation disrupts PU.1-mSWI/SNF binding, impairs complex targeting, and attenuates oncogenic transcription and proliferation in PU.1-dependent cancer cells. Dominant expression of the SWIFT domain in isolation sequesters TFs from mSWI/SNF and poisons TF-addicted cancer cells. Finally, TFs across diverse families interact with SMARCD paralog-specific SWIFT domains. These results define a major mechanism of cell type- and disease-specific mSWI/SNF chromatin targeting and inform approaches toward therapeutic modulation.

papers.llm_research_question_zh: 哺乳动物SWI/SNF染色质重塑复合体如何通过特异性分子界面与转录因子结合以实现基因组靶向，以及这一机制能否被用于干预转录因子依赖性肿瘤？
llm_highlights_zh: 鉴定出位于SMARCD亚基上的保守Ig样结构域SWIFT，作为SWI/SNF复合体的转录因子结合平台。
SWIFT对与PU.1转录激活结构域的直接结合具有必要性和充分性；单氨基酸突变可破坏该相互作用并削弱复合体靶向。
独立表达SWIFT结构域可竞争性隔离转录因子并抑制TF依赖性癌细胞，提示其具有治疗调控潜力。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 该研究报道了SWI/SNF复合体中一个特异性的Ig样转录因子结合结构域SWIFT，阐明其介导转录因子招募、染色质靶向及肿瘤相关转录程序维持的关键作用。
papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 哺乳动物SWI/SNF染色质重塑复合体可调控DNA可及性和基因表达，但其基因组靶向机制仍未被充分阐明。本文鉴定出SWIFT（SWI/SNF Ig-fold for transcription factor interactions），这是位于SMARCD亚基上的一个保守性转录因子结合平台。SWIFT对于与PU.1转录激活结构域的直接结合既是必需的也是充分的。单个氨基酸突变可破坏PU.1与哺乳动物SWI/SNF复合体的结合，损害复合体靶向，并减弱PU.1依赖性癌细胞中的致癌性转录和增殖。单独优势表达SWIFT结构域可从哺乳动物SWI/SNF复合体中隔离转录因子，从而对转录因子依赖性癌细胞产生抑制作用。最后，来自不同家族的转录因子均可与SMARCD旁系同源亚型特异性的SWIFT结构域相互作用。这些结果揭示了哺乳动物SWI/SNF染色质靶向在细胞类型和疾病特异性中的一种主要机制，并为治疗性调控策略提供了依据。

all_papers.title: Targeting modulated vascular smooth muscle cells in atherosclerosis via FAP-directed immunotherapy
all_papers.abstract: Vascular smooth muscle cell (VSMC) diversification drives atherosclerotic coronary artery disease (CAD). Mechanisms governing these cell state transitions remain unclear. We applied multiomic single-cell profiling, epitope mapping, and spatial transcriptomics across 27 human coronary arteries, identifying fibroblast activation protein (FAP) as a marker of modulated VSMCs. Lineage tracing in mice indicated that FAP+ cells originate from Myh11+ VSMCs, and FAP PET imaging in CAD patients showed plaque uptake. FAP+ cells states resided in the macrophage-rich neo-intima. Therapeutically, we developed an anti-FAP bispecific T-cell engager, which reduced plaque burden and remodeled the stromal-immune microenvironment through T-cell clonal expansion. Our study delivers a single-cell and spatial atlas of human CAD, establishes FAP as a marker of modulated VSMCs, and highlights immunotherapy for lipid-independent targets.

all_papers.llm_title_zh: 靶向动脉粥样硬化中调节型血管平滑肌细胞的FAP导向免疫治疗
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 靶向动脉粥样硬化中调节型血管平滑肌细胞的FAP导向免疫治疗 / Targeting modulated vascular smooth muscle cells in atherosclerosis via FAP-directed immunotherapy
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 在动脉粥样硬化性冠状动脉疾病中，血管平滑肌细胞状态转变由何种细胞群体和分子标志所表征，以及是否可通过靶向FAP阳性调节型VSMC的免疫治疗来减轻斑块负荷并重塑病灶微环境？
llm_highlights_zh: 多组学单细胞谱系分析与空间转录组学在27份人冠状动脉样本中鉴定出FAP为调节型血管平滑肌细胞的标志物。
小鼠谱系追踪显示FAP阳性细胞来源于Myh11阳性血管平滑肌细胞，且FAP PET在冠状动脉疾病患者斑块中显示摄取信号。
抗FAP双特异性T细胞连接器可降低斑块负荷，并通过促进T细胞克隆扩增重塑基质-免疫微环境。

all_papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 血管平滑肌细胞（VSMC）的多样化推动了动脉粥样硬化性冠状动脉疾病（CAD）的发生发展，但调控这些细胞状态转变的机制仍不清楚。研究者对27份人冠状动脉样本开展多组学单细胞分析、表位定位和空间转录组学研究，鉴定出成纤维细胞活化蛋白（FAP）是调节型VSMC的标志物。小鼠谱系追踪显示，FAP阳性细胞来源于Myh11阳性VSMC；在CAD患者中，FAP PET成像可见斑块摄取。FAP阳性细胞状态主要位于富含巨噬细胞的新内膜区域。在治疗方面，研究构建了一种抗FAP双特异性T细胞连接器，可降低斑块负荷，并通过诱导T细胞克隆扩增重塑基质-免疫微环境。该研究构建了人类CAD的单细胞与空间图谱，确立了FAP作为调节型VSMC标志物的意义，并提示针对非脂质靶点的免疫治疗具有潜力。

all_papers.primary_summary_raw: Vascular smooth muscle cell (VSMC) diversification drives atherosclerotic coronary artery disease (CAD). Mechanisms governing these cell state transitions remain unclear. We applied multiomic single-cell profiling, epitope mapping, and spatial transcriptomics across 27 human coronary arteries, identifying fibroblast activation protein (FAP) as a marker of modulated VSMCs. Lineage tracing in mice indicated that FAP+ cells originate from Myh11+ VSMCs, and FAP PET imaging in CAD patients showed plaque uptake. FAP+ cells states resided in the macrophage-rich neo-intima. Therapeutically, we developed an anti-FAP bispecific T-cell engager, which reduced plaque burden and remodeled the stromal-immune microenvironment through T-cell clonal expansion. Our study delivers a single-cell and spatial atlas of human CAD, establishes FAP as a marker of modulated VSMCs, and highlights immunotherapy for lipid-independent targets.

all_papers.title: Concurrent L1 retrotransposition events promote reciprocal translocations in human tumorigenesis
all_papers.abstract: LINE-1 (L1) retrotransposition generates somatic genomic variation in human cancer, but short-read sequencing has limited our understanding of its structural consequences and dynamics. Using long-read sequencing, we analyzed 10 tumors with exceptionally high retrotransposition activity, comprising over 6,000 somatic events. We reveal that L1-mediated reciprocal translocations occur frequently, typically driven by two concurrent L1 retrotransposition events on non-homologous chromosomes. Using an independent tumor cohort spanning low to high L1 activity, we estimate that retrotransposon-mediated rearrangements arise at a frequency of one event per 60 somatic retrotranspositions. Molecular timing analyses indicate that these events arise early in tumorigenesis, establishing L1 activity as an early driver of chromosomal instability. Our findings demonstrate that L1 contributes substantially to cancer genome evolution in certain tumors.

all_papers.title: A SWI/SNF-specific Ig-like domain, SWIFT, is a transcription factor binding platform
all_papers.abstract: Mammalian SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complexes modulate DNA accessibility and gene expression; however, their genomic targeting mechanisms remain incompletely understood. Here, we identify SWIFT (SWI/SNF Ig-fold for transcription factor interactions), a conserved transcription factor (TF) binding platform on the SMARCD subunits. SWIFT is necessary and sufficient for direct engagement with the transactivation domain of PU.1. A single amino acid mutation disrupts PU.1-mSWI/SNF binding, impairs complex targeting, and attenuates oncogenic transcription and proliferation in PU.1-dependent cancer cells. Dominant expression of the SWIFT domain in isolation sequesters TFs from mSWI/SNF and poisons TF-addicted cancer cells. Finally, TFs across diverse families interact with SMARCD paralog-specific SWIFT domains. These results define a major mechanism of cell type- and disease-specific mSWI/SNF chromatin targeting and inform approaches toward therapeutic modulation.

SWIFT对与PU.1转录激活结构域的直接结合具有必要性和充分性；单氨基酸突变可破坏该相互作用并削弱复合体靶向。
独立表达SWIFT结构域可竞争性隔离转录因子并抑制TF依赖性癌细胞，提示其具有治疗调控潜力。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 该研究报道了SWI/SNF复合体中一个特异性的Ig样转录因子结合结构域SWIFT，阐明其介导转录因子招募、染色质靶向及肿瘤相关转录程序维持的关键作用。
all_papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 哺乳动物SWI/SNF染色质重塑复合体可调控DNA可及性和基因表达，但其基因组靶向机制仍未被充分阐明。本文鉴定出SWIFT（SWI/SNF Ig-fold for transcription factor interactions），这是位于SMARCD亚基上的一个保守性转录因子结合平台。SWIFT对于与PU.1转录激活结构域的直接结合既是必需的也是充分的。单个氨基酸突变可破坏PU.1与哺乳动物SWI/SNF复合体的结合，损害复合体靶向，并减弱PU.1依赖性癌细胞中的致癌性转录和增殖。单独优势表达SWIFT结构域可从哺乳动物SWI/SNF复合体中隔离转录因子，从而对转录因子依赖性癌细胞产生抑制作用。最后，来自不同家族的转录因子均可与SMARCD旁系同源亚型特异性的SWIFT结构域相互作用。这些结果揭示了哺乳动物SWI/SNF染色质靶向在细胞类型和疾病特异性中的一种主要机制，并为治疗性调控策略提供了依据。

all_papers.primary_summary_raw: Mammalian SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complexes modulate DNA accessibility and gene expression; however, their genomic targeting mechanisms remain incompletely understood. Here, we identify SWIFT (SWI/SNF Ig-fold for transcription factor interactions), a conserved transcription factor (TF) binding platform on the SMARCD subunits. SWIFT is necessary and sufficient for direct engagement with the transactivation domain of PU.1. A single amino acid mutation disrupts PU.1-mSWI/SNF binding, impairs complex targeting, and attenuates oncogenic transcription and proliferation in PU.1-dependent cancer cells. Dominant expression of the SWIFT domain in isolation sequesters TFs from mSWI/SNF and poisons TF-addicted cancer cells. Finally, TFs across diverse families interact with SMARCD paralog-specific SWIFT domains. These results define a major mechanism of cell type- and disease-specific mSWI/SNF chromatin targeting and inform approaches toward therapeutic modulation.

all_papers.title: Sensing and perturbing mammalian cell states with reprogrammable ADAR sensors (RADARS)
all_papers.summary: This protocol uses Reprogrammable Adenosine Deaminase Acting on RNA (ADAR) Sensors (RADARS) to robustly sense RNA transcripts inside eukaryotic cells, enabling detection of changes in gene expression or targeting and perturbation of specific mammalian cell types and states
all_papers.llm_title_zh: 利用可重编程ADAR传感器（RADARS）感知并扰动哺乳动物细胞状态
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 利用可重编程ADAR传感器（RADARS）感知并扰动哺乳动物细胞状态 / Sensing and perturbing mammalian cell states with reprogrammable ADAR sensors (RADARS)
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何利用可重编程ADAR传感器（RADARS）在哺乳动物细胞中检测RNA转录本，从而感知基因表达变化，并实现对特定细胞类型或细胞状态的靶向识别与干预？
llm_highlights_zh: RADARS协议利用可重编程ADAR传感器在真核细胞内稳健检测RNA转录本。
该方法可用于感知基因表达变化，并识别特定哺乳动物细胞类型与细胞状态。
RADARS还支持对目标细胞类型或状态进行靶向干预。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 本文介绍了一种基于可重编程腺苷脱氨酶作用于RNA（ADAR）传感器的实验方案，即RADARS。该方法能够在真核细胞内稳健地检测RNA转录本，用于感知基因表达变化，并可对特定哺乳动物细胞类型及其状态进行靶向识别与扰动。
all_papers.primary_summary: 本文介绍了一种基于可重编程腺苷脱氨酶作用于RNA（ADAR）传感器的实验方案，即RADARS。该方法能够在真核细胞内稳健地检测RNA转录本，用于感知基因表达变化，并可对特定哺乳动物细胞类型及其状态进行靶向识别与扰动。
all_papers.primary_summary_raw: This protocol uses Reprogrammable Adenosine Deaminase Acting on RNA (ADAR) Sensors (RADARS) to robustly sense RNA transcripts inside eukaryotic cells, enabling detection of changes in gene expression or targeting and perturbation of specific mammalian cell types and states
all_papers.primary_summary_zh: 本文介绍了一种基于可重编程腺苷脱氨酶作用于RNA（ADAR）传感器的实验方案，即RADARS。该方法能够在真核细胞内稳健地检测RNA转录本，用于感知基因表达变化，并可对特定哺乳动物细胞类型及其状态进行靶向识别与扰动。

all_papers.title: APEX-seq maps transcriptome-wide subcellular RNA localization in living cells
all_papers.summary: This Protocol describes the transcriptome-wide labeling of RNAs in a particular subcellular compartment using proximity biotinylation by localized APEX2 enzyme and subsequent enrichment and sequencing of these transcripts
all_papers.llm_title_zh: APEX-seq绘制活细胞中全转录组亚细胞RNA定位图谱
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: APEX-seq绘制活细胞中全转录组亚细胞RNA定位图谱 / APEX-seq maps transcriptome-wide subcellular RNA localization in living cells
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何利用局部定位的APEX2邻近生物素化策略，在活细胞特定亚细胞区室中实现转录组范围RNA定位的标记、富集与测序分析？
llm_highlights_zh: 介绍利用局部定位APEX2酶进行邻近生物素化的APEX-seq实验流程。
可在活细胞特定亚细胞区室中实现转录组范围RNA标记、富集与测序。
该方法用于绘制RNA在亚细胞层面的定位图谱。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 本方案描述了一种APEX-seq方法：通过定位于特定亚细胞区室的APEX2酶进行邻近生物素化，对该区室内RNA进行转录组范围标记，随后对这些转录本进行富集和测序。
all_papers.primary_summary: 本方案描述了一种APEX-seq方法：通过定位于特定亚细胞区室的APEX2酶进行邻近生物素化，对该区室内RNA进行转录组范围标记，随后对这些转录本进行富集和测序。
all_papers.primary_summary_raw: This Protocol describes the transcriptome-wide labeling of RNAs in a particular subcellular compartment using proximity biotinylation by localized APEX2 enzyme and subsequent enrichment and sequencing of these transcripts

all_papers.title: A unique CD4⁺ T cell subset expressing granzyme K is regulated by transcription factor EOMES and important for T cell-mediated intestinal inflammation
all_papers.summary: Xie et al. identify a distinct subset of CD4+ helper T cells expressing EOMES and granzyme K in both mice and human models of colitis
all_papers.llm_title_zh: 受转录因子EOMES调控并对T细胞介导的肠道炎症至关重要的表达颗粒酶K的独特CD4⁺ T细胞亚群
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 受转录因子EOMES调控并对T细胞介导的肠道炎症至关重要的表达颗粒酶K的独特CD4⁺ T细胞亚群 / A unique CD4⁺ T cell subset expressing granzyme K is regulated by transcription factor EOMES and important for T cell-mediated intestinal inflammation
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: EOMES调控的颗粒酶K阳性CD4⁺ T细胞亚群是否构成一种独特的辅助性T细胞类型，并在T细胞介导的肠道炎症中发挥关键作用？
llm_highlights_zh: 研究鉴定出一类表达EOMES与颗粒酶K的独特CD4⁺辅助性T细胞亚群。
该细胞亚群在小鼠及人类结肠炎模型中均可观察到，提示其与肠道炎症相关。
转录因子EOMES参与调控该亚群，并对T细胞介导的肠道炎症具有重要作用。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: Xie等在小鼠和人类结肠炎模型中鉴定出一类独特的CD4⁺辅助性T细胞亚群，其特征为表达EOMES和颗粒酶K。该研究提示，这一细胞群受转录因子EOMES调控，并在T细胞介导的肠道炎症中具有重要意义。
all_papers.primary_summary: Xie等在小鼠和人类结肠炎模型中鉴定出一类独特的CD4⁺辅助性T细胞亚群，其特征为表达EOMES和颗粒酶K。该研究提示，这一细胞群受转录因子EOMES调控，并在T细胞介导的肠道炎症中具有重要意义。
